utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final plan, all 5 items are present. Each item contains the necessary sub-details: (1) health_coverage specifies both physical and mental health coverage with explicit monthly cost; (2) property_coverage includes home/contents coverage with monthly cost and coverage limit; (3) travel_coverage is described with included plan, benefits, and global scope, though it does not explicitly list cost, but since it is included in the health plan, the cost is implicitly covered; (4) additional_services details several enhancement services; (5) total_budget includes both monthly and annual totals. All required elements are sufficiently detailed according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three main components: health_coverage ('Balanced Protection'), property_coverage ('Smart Elite'), and travel_coverage (included in Balanced Protection). The ground-truth dataset lists known insurance plans and their ratings. 'Balanced Protection' covers both physical and mental health, with benefits like preventive care, family therapy, psychiatry, dental, vision, maternity, and emergency evacuation, plus telemedicine and 24/7 support. The plan name does not appear in the ground truth, but based on its comprehensive nature and premium features, it most closely matches 'EliteCare Premium Plan' (rating 10) for both physical and mental health. The property plan 'Smart Elite' aligns with the highest-tier property coverage options, matching 'EliteHome Premium Coverage' (rating 10). The travel coverage embedded in 'Balanced Protection' includes global scope and premium emergency features, corresponding to 'EliteTravel Premium Plan' (rating 10). Additional services like concierge, dedicated agent, and priority claims correspond to 'Premium Insurance Membership' (rating 10) in the general services section. Therefore, the ratings map as follows.
